LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO DISMISS PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO DISMISS PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT WILL BE GRANTED IF PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PROPERLY FILE AND SERVE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ON MAY 10, 2018, THE COURT SUSTAINED THE CITY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT, WITH LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT BY JULY 10, 2018. PLAINTIFF ASSERTS IN HIS LATE-FILED OPPOSITION THAT THE AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS FILED WITH THE COURT IN A TIMELY MANNER. IT WAS NOT. ON JULY 10, 2018, PLAINTIFF FILED A "DECLARATION," WHICH ATTACHED A PROPOSED "FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT." HOWEVER, AS THE CITY'S COUNSEL TWICE INFORMED PLAINTIFF IN WRITING, THAT DECLARATION DOES NOT CONSTITUTE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, WHICH MUST BE SEPARATELY FILED WITH THE COURT. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE UNTIL MAY 10, 2019, TO FILE AND SERVE HIS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. IF PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DO SO, THE COURT WILL GRANT THE CITY'S MOTION TO DISMISS THE ACTION WITH PREJUDICE WITHOUT THE NEED FOR FURTHER HEARING OR BRIEFING. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-31-2019 Continued To Dec-24-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2019 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Renee E. Rosenblit, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, T:415.554.3853, for defendant/moving party;
Charles Pitts, T:415.368.2354, plaintiff/opposing party, appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: Plaintiff contested the tentative ruling. The matter was argued. After arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO HUMAN SERVICES' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT:

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to plaintiff's second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's late-filed one-page "Opposition to Demure [sic]" does not contain any legal argument or, for that matter, any content at all, other than the bare fact that Plaintiff opposes the demurrer. Plaintiff is not entitled to special consideration because of his self-represented status. (See ViaView, Inc. v. Retzlaff (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 198, 208.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-23-2019 Continued To Nov-05-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-16-2019 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Charles Pitts, 415-368-2354 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Renee Rosenblit, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3800 of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING - ARGUED. The Court asks Plaintiff why he did not respond to the Demurrer. Plaintiff requests 60 days to amend complaint. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Plaintff has 30 days leave to amend. If the City Demurrers to that Complaint, Plaintiff needs to file a timely response to the Demurrer 9 court days before the hearing date. If not, the Court will sustain the Demurrer without leave to amend and the action will be over.

Defendant's Counsel to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff'S Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Continued To May-10-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Renee E. rosenblit, Esq., Decuty City Attorney, CCSF, Telephone 415-554-3853, appearing for Defendant(s) CCSF et al;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff'S Complaint.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to dismiss plaintiff's complaint will be granted if Plaintiff does not properly file and serve a First Amended Complaint. On May 10, 2018, the Court sustained the City's demurrer to Plaintiff's complaint, with leave to file a First Amended Complaint by July 10, 2018. Plaintiff asserts in his late-filed opposition that the amended complaint was filed with the court in a timely manner. It was not. On July 10, 2018, Plaintiff filed a "Declaration," which attached a proposed "First Amended Complaint." However, as the City's counsel twice informed Plaintiff in writing, that Declaration does not constitute a first amended complaint, which must be separately filed with the Court. Plaintiff has leave until May 10, 2019, to file and serve his first amended complaint. If Plaintiff fails to do so, the Court will grant the City's motion to dismiss the action with prejudice without the need for further hearing or briefing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
